description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, natural appearance. Its surface is predominantly a mottled mix of reddish-brown and gray tones, with some areas showing lighter speckling and subtle marbling. The rock has a coarse, uneven texture with visible bumps, crevices, and angular edges, suggesting it was naturally weathered rather than cut or shaped. One side appears flatter and lighter in color, while the other sides are more rounded and darker, with deeper indentations. The overall form is roughly triangular when viewed from above, with no sharp points but several pronounced ridges. This rock would blend seamlessly into an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A rugged, reddish-brown and gray rock with a coarse, irregular surface and angular, weathered edges. Its mottled coloration and natural form make it suitable for urban landscaping or as a barrier.
description_view_0: This view shows a broad, rounded face with deep brown marbling and pronounced surface undulations.
description_view_1: Here, the rock appears taller and more vertical, with a darker, more shadowed side and visible surface pitting.
description_view_2: This side is flatter and lighter in color, with more visible speckling and a less pronounced texture.
description_view_3: The rock's edge is visible, showing a transition from the lighter, flatter face to the darker, more rugged side.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside area
